primary_factors: Primary hazard is patchy ice/black ice from trace–very light freezing drizzle/freezing rain roughly 12 AM–6 AM with temps ~19–22°F (ice risk is disproportionately disruptive vs. QPF)
No extreme-cold trigger: wind chills around 8–12°F at bus time are well above Michigan closure thresholds
No accumulating snow signal (most sources show ~0"; one public source suggests <1" which is not closure-level by itself in Michigan)
Infrastructure reality: highways/major primaries likely treated and passable by 6–8 AM, but secondary/local neighborhood roads may remain slick into bus routes (key limiter for Rockford)
News/decision signals are quiet: no confirmed neighboring-district closures/delays and no strong community buzz; that usually correlates with ‘open’ or at most a delay
Power is a low-likelihood wildcard for Rockford: statewide outages are high but Kent County appears essentially unaffected right now, reducing odds of a weather-forced closure due to outages
decision_rationale: Across the six inputs, there’s broad agreement this is not a snow-accumulation event and not an extreme-cold closure setup. The meaningful risk is a nuisance-ice scenario: very light freezing rain/drizzle overnight with temps in the low 20s, which can create patchy black ice—especially on bridges, ramps, hills, and lower-priority subdivision/secondary roads. That aligns with the Safety and Infrastructure assessments: primaries should be mostly serviceable by 6–8 AM with active treatment, while local streets may remain slick into the bus window. Historically, that pattern more often yields ‘open’ or a short delay rather than a full closure, and the News Intel scan shows no confirmed neighboring-district closures/delays and no strong community buzz—both of which typically push decisions toward staying open. Power-grid risk appears lower for Rockford specifically (Kent County currently near-zero outages), so a power-driven closure is not the base case. Net: closure is plausible if early-morning glaze is more widespread than modeled or if bus test routes fail on secondaries, but the most likely outcome remains OPEN, with a meaningful chance of a 1–2 hour delay. Therefore I set a 25% probability for a full snow day/closure (with a higher probability for a delay than for closure).

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 2 round(s) with 10.0% spread. Average probability: 21%.
